title: pyrus × delacourii
intro: Pyrus × delacourii, also known as the Chinese pear or Delacour's pear, is a hybrid plant species resulting from the cross between Pyrus communis and Pyrus bretschneideri. This plant is primarily cultivated for its fruit, which is rich in medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to aid in digestion, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antibacterial and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Pyrus × delacourii include reducing fever, alleviating coughs, and promoting relaxation, making it a valuable ingredient in traditional Chinese medicine. Herbal preparations of Pyrus × delacourii can be found in various forms, including dried fruit, tea, and extracts, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, gastrointestinal problems, and skin conditions.
